video_title: Long Story Short | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
Wow, this was SO good! I only ever watched like a few episodes of BoJack, so I came into this fresh. I loved every episode, and was sad when I reached the tenth episode. My favorite set of lines is in episode 0:03 5 when Avi and Yoshi are talking about genetics, using condiments as examples, and Yoshi asks why he's the creamer, Avi retorts that he knows why. You need to watch the show to understand the joke, but OMG that made me giggle. I don't think there was a single character I disliked... other than perhaps the uncle and Yoshi's childhood best friend, who reminded me far too much of my own brother's childhood best friend.
Also, what's with the show creator and making dudes born in 1991 just kind of a little off the wall, even if in different ways? BoJack brought us Todd, who can never be outdone, but then Long Story Short brought us Yoshi, who's definitely a character. I can't decide which of the three siblings was my favorite, honestly.

video_title: Long Story Short | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: So I am a little more than halfway through the show, and as a fellow Bojack fan I wanna say for any other Horseman fans that haven’t seen this yet: it’s really good, but a little cozier and nostalgic. It has very real moments, but not near as big gut punches and introspections. It’s more just a real but much lighter and fun representation of a family. It indulges a lot more in light hearted family revelations and is very relatable. If someone is looking for 100 puns a minute alongside the most depressing and poignant emotional gut punches, this show may not scratch that itch fully. But it’s super sweet and relatable and still very funny with very real and grounded moments. Highly recommend.
